text: Some creatures deceive to survive. How? Meet Batesian mimicry, where harmless species imitate dangerous ones. This strategy confuses predators, increasing survival chances. Take the viceroy butterfly; it resembles the toxic monarch, tricking birds. But is all mimicry effective? Not always—environment plays a key role. Want more mind-bending nature facts? Hit follow!
